content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of size NNN.

Find a pair of indices (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) such that:


1‚Č§i,j‚Č§N,i‚Č†j1 \le i, j \le N, i \ne j1‚Č§i,j‚Č§N,iÓÄ†=j;
Ai+AjA_i + A_jAi‚Äč+Aj‚Äč is not prime‚Ä†^{\dagger}‚Ä†.
In case multiple (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) pairs are valid, you can print any one. If it is impossible to find such (i,j)(i, j)(i,j), output ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1 instead.

‚Ä†^{\dagger}‚Ä† A number x (x‚Č•2)x \ (x \ge 2)x (x‚Č•2) is said to be prime if it has exactly 222 positive divisors - 111, and xxx itself.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line contains NNN - the size of the array AAA.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers - A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč, the elements of the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output:



Two space-separated integers iii and jjj, denoting a valid pair (i,j)(i, j)(i,j).
‚ąí1-1‚ąí1 if there exist no valid pair (i,j)(i, j)(i,j).

In case multiple (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) pairs are valid, you can print any one.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111 : A1+A2=9A_1 + A_2 = 9A1‚Äč+A2‚Äč=9 which is not prime .
Test case 222 : A1+A3=4A_1 + A_3 = 4A1‚Äč+A3‚Äč=4 which is not prime. (2,4)(2, 4)(2,4) is also a valid answer.
Test case 333 : All values of Ai+AjA_i + A_jAi‚Äč+Aj‚Äč for (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) satisfying i‚Č†ji \ne jiÓÄ†=j are either 222 or 333, both of which are prime. Hence, the answer is ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1.
